_meta.doc: §13 P4 V-axis F-layer (frontend render) live proofs — Playwright MCP + postgres, the §13.4 combo. Each entry asserts a rendered [data-rag-tile] .sc-hero value EQUALS the DB canonical value for the active hive. Falsifiable: journey_vaxis.py reads this to mark a journey's F cell attributed iff its entry has ok=true; delete this file and those F cells degrade to pending. Re-prove by signing in (Leandro / Baguio 9b4eaeac) and reading the tiles.
_meta.method: read [data-rag-tile] .sc-hero (NOT body-regex); wait AFTER the page's one self-reload then bare evaluate; VERIFY-FIRST same-named != same-derivation (due_soon excludes already-overdue assets at the ASSET level).

components.name: open WOs (logbook.status='Open')
components.db_query: count(*) v_logbook_truth WHERE hive AND status='Open'

J1.composite: open_issues 24 == 10 open WOs + 10 PM overdue + 4 stock — rendered composite equals the sum of its DB canonical parts

tiles.db_query: DISTINCT due_soon assets MINUS DISTINCT overdue assets (asset-level exclusion) — naive row-level count is 27; the page correctly excludes the 7 also-overdue assets
count(*) marketplace_listings WHERE section='parts' AND status='published' (marketplace spans hives)

J4.derivation: page renders the most-recent HISTORY_LIMIT=80 entries (voice-journal.html:401 .limit(HISTORY_LIMIT)) auth-scoped to the signed-in user (auth_uid e701ea32…, who has 11,933 ≥ 80 → cap correctly exercised)
J4.verify_first_note: same-named != same-derivation — by-design pagination (community pattern), NOT a count bug; the 80 == the LIMIT, entries are auth-scoped
J4.ok_basis: rendered count == HISTORY_LIMIT and entries are auth-scoped (D/AU/S proven at DB level)

J5.render: OPEN ISSUES 24 (10 open WOs + 10 PM overdue + 4 stock)
J5.tenancy_proof: the signed-in Baguio (9b4eaeac) supervisor sees ONLY Baguio-scoped values — every rendered component equals the Baguio DB canonical; no other hive's data appears in the UI (the F-layer end of cross-hive isolation)
J5.ok_basis: rendered == active-hive DB canonical; cross-hive data absent from the render

J6.render: 10/10 probed surfaces healthy (100%) · per-surface HTTP 200 + deps (supabase/groq/cerebras ✓)
J6.derivation: status.html renders the live /health SLO grid (Pillar O) — matches the live /health probes journey_vaxis asserts for the A/AV cells
J6.ok_basis: SLO grid renders live health == the /health endpoint responses

tiles.db_query: count(*) v_risk_truth WHERE risk_level IN ('critical','high') (hot assets)
count(*) v_risk_truth WHERE risk_level IN ('critical','high') (1 crit + 1 high of 5)

rendered_live_but_derivation_unverified.note: the svc's ISO-22400 partial OEE (availability×quality); analytics_snapshots.payload shows per-asset availability 93-97% + oee_pct=null (Performance dim unconfigured) — 86% is a fleet computation NOT cleanly traced to a single canonical → value-derivation verification pending

T_analytics.ok_basis: the PM-compliance tile == the proven RPC (nerve #5); credited on that verified tile + the resolved env-block. OEE/MTBF render live but are NOT claimed verified (verify-first — no false proof).

T_ai_quality.journey: terminus render (P-axis attribution) — VALUE-DERIVATION proof of the page's own aggregate()
T_ai_quality.method: called the page's REAL aggregate(costRows, feedbackRows) fn via Playwright with a deterministic fixture (3 cost rows + 3 feedback) and asserted EVERY output against an independent hand-derived oracle — no DB-tie fragility (the live path .limit(2000)s + orders by created_at, so a separate psql window can differ; this proves the DERIVATION itself).
T_ai_quality.real_sources: ai_cost_log (12,499 live rows for the hive, 30d) + ai_reply_feedback — hive-scoped reads confirmed in code (L399/L417, .eq('hive_id', HIVE_ID)); page renders these via aggregate().
T_ai_quality.nerve_basis: ai-quality aggregate() value-derivation proven via the REAL page fn (deterministic fixture, 10/10 metrics incl. the honest ROI formula + schema-compliance ratio + per-fn cost-sort match the hand oracle) + reads real hive-scoped ai_cost_log/ai_reply_feedback — the page's compute is value-correct
T_ai_quality.verify_first_note: did NOT credit via a live tile==DB render (the .limit(2000)+created_at-tie boundary makes a separate psql window non-deterministic → would risk a false match); proved the DERIVATION deterministically instead
